mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, driving immediate positive equity returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improved manufacturing capacity utilization, triggering short-term momentum buying in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during tightening cycles, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as fundamentals improve.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and forces immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high inflation readings, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines, or the interaction effect is negative in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.4 million units signal a construction boom that multiplies through supply chains and financial services, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST readings above the threshold consistently precede negative 60-day returns, or the signal information coefficient turns negative during housing market corrections.
